steps.title: Identify the key contrast
steps.content: The crucial signal is the absence-to-presence shift: neither History nor works predating it mention the Round Table, yet Malory's Le Morte d'Arthur includes it. The passage also notes that only many (not all) of Malory's sources derive from Geoffrey's History, meaning he used other sources as well.

steps.title: Apply this to the answer choices
steps.content:
Because the Round Table appears in Malory's work but not in Geoffrey's or any earlier source, Malory must have found it in a source that Geoffrey was unfamiliar with—one of his non-Geoffrey sources. The other choices either misstate text similarities, contradict the source evidence, or conflate unrelated concepts.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
